channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Guardiola says 'nothing is eternal' as Man City exit confirmed
item.description: Manchester City confirm that Pep Guardiola will leave at the end of the season after a decade of unparalleled success.

item.title: 'Tuchel's gambles and iron conviction position him as the anti-Southgate'
item.description: Thomas Tuchel proves he is the "anti-Southgate" in making tough calls for England's World Cup 2026 squad.

item.title: Foden, Palmer, Alexander-Arnold to miss World Cup
item.description: Phil Foden, Cole Palmer and Trent Alexander-Arnold have not been included in Thomas Tuchel's 26-man England squad for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Carrick confirmed as Man Utd permanent boss
item.description: Manchester United confirm the appointment of Michael Carrick as their permanent head coach on a two-year contract.

item.title: Why genius Guardiola is the greatest coach of all time
item.description: BBC pundit Micah Richards explains why English football is going to miss Pep Guardiola after his exit from Manchester City.

item.title: How Guardiola transformed the Premier League tactically
item.description: Pep Guardiola has changed the tactical landscape in the Premier League, but he has also adapted to its challenges.

item.title: Sutton's final-day predictions v Sam Tompkins
item.description: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on singer-songwriter Sam Tompkins, plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for Sunday's final round of Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Slot refuses to confirm Salah selected for final game
item.description: Arne Slot refuses to confirm whether Mohamed Salah will play a part at Anfield on Sunday, in what would be his last appearance for Liverpool.

item.title: Southampton's Eckert backed spying as club conduct deemed 'deplorable'
item.description: Southampton's spying was authorised by head coach Tonda Eckert and was a "contrived and determined plan from the top down to gain a competitive advantage", an independent disciplinary panel says.

item.title: From Benidorm beaches to Barcelona - the rise of Lopez
item.description: With the Women's Champions League final between Barcelona and Lyon on Saturday, BBC Sport takes a look at the extraordinary rise of football's next global superstar, Vicky Lopez.

item.title: Guardiola's legacy of genius will echo through all ages for years to come
item.description: As Manchester City prepare for Pep Guardiola's exit, Phil McNulty reflects on how he has changed football in England.

item.title: Eleven players who defined Guardiola's Man City era
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at 11 players who had their careers shaped by Pep Guardiola during his 10 season as at Manchester City.

item.title: How Palmer and Foden lost ruthless Tuchel's battle for number 10 roles
item.description: Two years ago Phil Foden and Cole Palmer were England's future. Now, they cannot even get into the squad amid fierce rivalry for the number 10 role.

item.title: Ultimate image for Saudi Arabia's football poster boy: Ronaldo wins title
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo wins his first league title with Al-Nassr after three years in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Thousands welcome home triumphant Villa as heroes
item.description: A sea of claret and blue floods Birmingham as fans celebrate the team's Europa League win.

item.title: Stapleton and Nolan return for crucial qualifiers
item.description: Jessie Stapleton and Hayley Nolan return to the Republic of Ireland squad for the upcoming World Cup qualifiers against Netherlands and France.

item.title: Quiz: Name all the Championship play-off final winners
item.description: To give you a hand we've listed the number of times, and the years they have done it in. Good luck!

item.title: The games that defined Guardiola's Premier League titles
item.description: BBC Sport looks back at the defining moments of Pep Guardiola's six Premier League titles, following reports that he is set to depart Manchester City after Sunday's final game of the season.

item.title: Tuchel explains England World Cup squad selections
item.description: England boss Thomas Tuchel said his job "isn't necessarily to select the 26 most talented players" as he explained his World Cup squad selections.

item.title: What's your favourite Football Focus moment?
item.description: The longest-running football magazine show in the world comes to an end this Sunday after 52 years and here are some of our favourite moments.

item.title: Watch Partick Thistle & St Mirren fight out play-off draw
item.description: Watch the best of the action as Partick Thistle and St Mirren fight out a draw in the first leg of the Scottish Premiership play-off final.

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League as Man City held at Bournemouth
item.description: Arsenal are crowned champions of the Premier League for the first time in 22 years after Manchester City are held to a 1-1 draw at Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium, a result which secures European football for the home side for the first time in their history.

item.title: From Chelsea relegation to £200m game - how play-offs have evolved
item.description: From crowds of 15,000 watching on 40 years ago to the richest game in world football today, the play-offs have changed significantly.

item.title: Chelsea to rival Arsenal for Kroupi - Friday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea join Arsenal in the race for Eli Junior Kroupi, Fulham and Everton eye Harry Wilson, Manchester United want Sandro Tonali and Ederson, plus more.

item.title: All your FPL stats and Premier League team news in one place
item.description: Every key bit of Premier League team news and the most important Fantasy Premier League statistics - all in one place.

item.title: Everton's Ndiaye adamant Senegal are champions of Africa
item.description: Iliman Ndiaye insists Senegal are African champions in "many people's eyes" despite a legal wrangle over the ultimate destination of the 2025 Afcon title.

item.title: What is at stake on final day of Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what is at stake on the final day of the 2025-26 Premier League season.

item.title: Arteta told of title win by crying son - while having barbecue
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says he could not watch the game that secured the Premier League title, opting to have a barbecue in his garden instead.

item.title: Wales boss Wilkinson signs contract extension
item.description: Wales head coach Rhian Wilkinson signs a two-year contract extension which will keep her in the role until 2029.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Every nation's squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the United States, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: Boro boss relieved 'right thing done' over Spygate
item.description: Kim Hellberg reflects on a whirlwind of emotions on Middlesbrough's reinstatement for the Championship play-offs following Southampton's expulsion.

item.title: Spygate: Who is Tonda Eckert and can he keep his job?
item.description: Tonda Eckert was a head coach who had the world at his feet. After Spygate, he may be fighting for his job and his future in the game.

item.title: Odegaard named captain as King of Norway announces squad
item.description: Arsenal captain Martin Odegaard is named captain of Norway's 26-man squad for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: In, or out? The joy and despair of World Cup squad selection
item.description: BBC Sport football pundits Micah Richards, Joe Hart, Theo Walcott, Stephen Warnock share their stories of what it is like to get a call before a major tournament telling you if you are in or out of the England squad.

item.title: Askou joins Toulouse - what now for Motherwell?
item.description: Jens Berthel Askou leaves Motherwell to become head coach of Toulouse - what now for the Fir Park club?

item.title: Tottenham lose at Chelsea to take relegation fight to final day
item.description: Tottenham miss the opportunity to secure Premier League safety after they're beaten 2-1 by Chelsea at Stamford Bridge, meaning their fight against relegation will go down to the final day, with the club sitting two points above the bottom three.

item.title: 'Exceptional' Arsenal celebrate as Premier League title is secured
item.description: Watch the moment Arsenal players and staff celebrate as they secure their first Premier League title in 22 years after Manchester City draw 1-1 at Bournemout

item.title: 'Let me talk to my chairman' - Guardiola coy on future after Bournemouth draw
item.description: Pep Guardiola remained tightlipped on his future after Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth - a result that sees Arsenal win the Premier League for the first time in 22 years.

item.title: Going, going, gone... what happens at Man City when Guardiola departs?
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart discuss what Manchester City do now after missing out on the Premier League title, along with strong reports that manager Pep Guardiola will leave the club in the summer.

item.title: Runners-up no more - How Arsenal's patience paid off with Arteta
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart praise Arsenal for giving Mikel Arteta time after he guided the club to their first Premier League title in 22 years, following Manchester City's 1-1 draw with Bournemouth.

item.title: 'So much on the line' - Spurs struggles sets up final day relegation decider
item.description: Joe Hart and Danny Murphy discuss how Tottenham's lack of clincal finishing saw them miss an opportunity to secure Premier League safety and analyse who will avoid relegation with Spurs and West Ham in danger of going down on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: 'I can't ask for more' - Iraola delighted as Bournemouth secure Europe
item.description: Andoni Iraola says "he couldn't ask for more" as Bournemouth secure European football for the first time in their history with a 1-1 draw against Manchester City. The game was the Spaniard's final home match in charge, before he leaves in the summer.

item.title: 'They've deserved it' - How brilliant Bournemouth reached Europe - analysis
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart praise Bournemouth's tactics and attitude as their 1-1 draw against Manchester City secures European football for the first time in their club's history.

item.title: Head coach to manager? Chelsea's key change puts Alonso in control
item.description: Speaking on Match of the Day, Danny Murphy and Joe Hart analyse Chelsea's decision to appoint Xabi Alonso.

item.title: Premier League winner on Tuesday, GCSEs on Thursday
item.description: Max Dowman is the youngest player in Premier League history and had to take his GCSE exams just days after sealing the title.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - McLean v Gemmill
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: 'Still place for older gentleman' - O'Neill coy on Celtic future
item.description: Martin O'Neill says there is still a place in football management for "older gentlemen" as he keeps the possibility of extending his Celtic stay open.

item.title: Vote for your favourite moment from 52 years of Football Focus
item.description: Vote for which Football Focus moment you want to be shown again in the programme's final episode on Sunday.

item.title: Neuer, 40, reverses retirement to be Germany's first-choice World Cup keeper
item.description: Bayern Munich goalkeeper Manuel Neuer comes out of international retirement to be named in Germany's squad for the 2026 World Cup, with manager Julian Nagelsmann saying the 40-year-old will be his team's number one for the tournament.

item.title: Hull City 'collateral damage' in Spygate saga
item.description: Hull City boss Sergej Jakirovic says the EFL's decision to switch play-off opponents has left his side's preperations in limbo.

item.title: Rogers named Europa League Player of the Season
item.description: Aston Villa midfielder Morgan Rogers is named the Europa League Player of the Season after helping his side win the tournament.

item.title: What would Crystal Palace winning Conference League mean for English clubs in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how Crystal Palace winning the Conference League would effect English teams entering Europe next season

item.title: Stories, scheduling & spectacle - five takeaways from 2025-26 WSL
item.description: With the 2025-26 season in the books, BBC Sport answers five pertinent questions from the Women's Super League.

item.title: What tactics might Alonso use at Chelsea?
item.description: What tactical approach will Xabi Alonso bring to Stamford Bridge next season after being announced as Chelsea boss?

item.title: Boats, fire and a TikTok song - inside Arsenal's title win
item.description: The inside story of how Mikel Arteta turned Arsenal from 'nearly men' into Premier League winners.

item.title: The key moments that decided the Premier League title race
item.description: BBC Sport looks at the key moments that helped turn Arsenal from nearly men to league winners.

item.title: Meet the 26 players in Scotland’s first men's World Cup squad since 1998
item.description: Boss Steve Clarke has named his squad for this summer's World Cup - meet the 26 players.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: What does Premiership climax mean for European places?
item.description: What does Saturday's dramatic and chaotic climax to the Scottish Premiership top-six mean for European football next season?

item.title: All you need to know about the Europa League final
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at all you need to know before this year's Europa League final.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: SFA 'fully supports' referee Robertson & releases Celtic Park audio
item.description: The Scottish FA say match officials took the "correct action in ending the game" in Saturday's dramatic final-day Scottish Premiership title decider between Celtic and Hearts.

item.title: McTominay likely to sign new Napoli deal - gossip
item.description: Scotland star poised to extend Serie A stay, while Burnley step up their pursuit of Celtic-linked manager

item.title: 'Underdogs bite' - Dunfermline boss Lennon gives Celtic cup final warning
item.description: Neil Lennon warns former club Celtic that "underdogs bite" as his Dunfermline Athletic side aim to cause a Scottish Cup shock in Saturday's final.

item.title: Liverpool agree deal with Slovenia defender Agrez
item.description: Liverpool agree a deal to sign Cologne defender Sara Agrez on a free transfer on 1 July.

item.title: After firing Man City to title - is Shaw best striker in WSL history?
item.description: After Khadija Shaw helped Manchester City win the Women's Super League with another superb season, BBC Sport looks at where she stands among the division's greatest strikers.

item.title: North Korean side wins in South to reach final
item.description: A team of North Korean footballers who made history by crossing the border into South Korea earned a victory to take them into the Asian Women's Champions League final.

item.title: Emslie recalled by Scotland five months after giving birth
item.description: Scotland have recalled Claire Emslie for next month's Women's World Cup qualifying double-header against Israel, five months on from the forward giving birth.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
